title: FEE_DEPOSIT
description: Message sent by Actor or Claimant to deposit fees into the Keeper's escrow. The Claimant SHOULD send this before or at the same time as ASSESSMENT_REQUEST, and it must be confirmed before the Referee issues a FEE_STATUS_QUERY. The Actor sends this after ACTOR_ACKNOWLEDGMENT. See RFC-0002 Section 1 for payment flow details.

depositor_id.description: Terminal ID of the depositing party (Actor or Claimant).

amount.description: Amount to deposit into escrow. The expected amount per role is declared in the Referee's REFEREE_PROFILE fee.deposit (default when none is declared: the requesting party bears the full fee.amount and the counterparty is NOT_REQUIRED; a declared allocation may share the cost between parties) — see STD-029. A deposit below the expected amount by the deadline is treated as NOT_DEPOSITED in fee_compliance (STD-026). Bounded to at most 3 decimal places (every ISO 4217 currency, including BHD/KWD/OMR) so JCS number serialization round-trips exactly across implementations.

timestamp.description: Timestamp of when this deposit message was sent.

signature.description: Ed25519 signature of the depositing party (depositor_id) over the canonical JSON of all fields excluding this field (STD-033). Verified by the Keeper against depositor_id's registered public_key before the escrow is credited.
